title: NIS2 OT Incident Detection — Process and Protocol Anomalies

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(f) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.39: NIS2 OT Incident Detection — Process and Protocol Anomalies.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.39' running on index=ot_protocol (HEC from Edge Hub or passive taps), nis2_ot_baseline.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=ot_network` `sourcetype=edge_hub:modbus` (Splunk Edge Hub collectors) carries `function_code`, `register_span`, `unit_id`, `plc_name`, `cell_line`. Companion `sourcetype=edge_hub:opcua` feeds `node_id`, `browse_depth` when OPC-UA pilots exist. Baselines stored in `nis2_ot_baseline.csv` with `expected_fn_variety`, `expected_span`. Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) routes notables to OT SOC queues; Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 5151) optional for asset context. Ingest uses HTTP Event Collector token on Heavy Forwarder per **Settings > Data inputs > HTTP Event Collector**.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 5151), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — Edge Hub forwarder), Splunk Edge Hub solution documentation
spl:
index=ot_network sourcetype=edge_hub:modbus earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(function_code) as fn_variety avg(register_span) as avg_span by plc_name cell_line
| lookup nis2_ot_baseline.csv cell_line OUTPUT expected_fn_variety expected_span
| eval anomaly=if(fn_variety > expected_fn_variety*2 OR avg_span > expected_span*3, 1, 0)
| where anomaly=1
| table plc_name, cell_line, fn_variety, expected_fn_variety, avg_span, expected_span, anomaly
description: Statistically compares Modbus traffic diversity and register span per PLC/cell to engineering-approved baselines so unusual industrial conversations—possible manipulation or spoofing—surface before they cascade into physical harm.
value: Gives safety and plant leadership audit-grade proof that operational technology is watched with the same seriousness as office systems, by storing baseline deviations with timestamps suitable for regulator briefings after near-misses.
implementation: (1) Capture baselines during golden runs with operations sign-off. (2) Land Edge Hub firehose on `index=ot_network`, never on security-only indexes. (3) Tune multipliers (`*2`, `*3`) with plant engineers. (4) Page OT SOC via ES (Splunkbase 263) with response SLAs. (5) Feed confirmed anomalies into incident ticketing (`snow:incident` via Splunkbase 1928 if adopted).
visualization: Line chart of `fn_variety`, table of anomalous cells, single value anomaly count, heatmap by shift.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Planned recipe experiments** — Distinguish: operations widen `function_code` mix for QA lots. Suppress: lookup `nis2_ot_change_window.csv` keyed on `cell_line` with `experiment=true` and `valid_until`.
• **Firmware downloads** — Distinguish: short spikes when vendor updates PLC. Suppress: time-bound exception register rows referencing `change_ticket`.
• **Simulator labs sharing production names** — Distinguish: lab `plc_name` collides with live tag. Suppress: append `| where NOT match(plc_name,"LAB$")` or segregate lab traffic to `index=ot_lab`.
• **Baseline CSV drift** — Distinguish: baseline stale after throughput upgrade. Suppress: enforce quarterly regeneration workflow; temporary widen multipliers via macro argument.
• **Passive tap packet loss** — Distinguish: span looks huge because mirrored span incomplete. Suppress: correlate with `interface_errors` from tap switch using side join before alerting.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Edge Hub or equivalent gateway splitting Modbus metadata (not blocking control frames) into JSON for HEC. Token restricted to `index=ot_network`.
• Heavy Forwarder on plant DMZ with **1 Gbps** mirror to tap; clock synchronised within **100 ms** of PLC NTP.
• OT Safety SME approves field dictionary (`function_code`, `register_span`).
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) with OT-specific notable queue.
• Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 5151) optional for enrichment macros.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Create HEC token `ot_edge_ingest`, set `indexes = ot_network`, `source type = edge_hub:modbus` in `inputs.conf` override. Validate with `index=ot_network | head 1` showing extractions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run SPL over rolling `earliest=-7d`. Save `UC-22.2.39`. Use adaptive response in ES to assign OT tier. OPC-UA variant: duplicate search with `sourcetype=edge_hub:opcua`, swap stats for `dc(node_id)`.

```spl
index=ot_network sourcetype=edge_hub:modbus earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(function_code) as fn_variety avg(register_span) as avg_span by plc_name cell_line
```

Understanding this SPL: anomalies compare live stats to CSV baselines—never thresholds alone—to respect each line’s physics.

Step 3 — Validate
During approved maintenance, inject known-good traffic and confirm search stays quiet. During drills, inject wider function mix and expect alert within **10 minutes**.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
If `lookup` misses, check `cell_line` spelling vs historian. If `register_span` null, update field_alias in `props.conf`.

Step 5 — Governance
Archive notable PDF + SPL snapshot to `audit_evidence`. Apply `evidenceSigning`. Review baselines quarterly with plant manager sign-off. Filter the SPL using `lookup nis2_ot_exception_register.csv` only after dual-control approval.
grandmaExplanation: We learn what "normal" chatter looks like on the factory floor networks, then we speak up when a line starts talking in an unusual way that could mean trouble—not just when office computers misbehave.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: `UC-22.2.39` results listing `plc_name`, `cell_line`, `anomaly`, plus Edge Hub sample payloads and ES notable IDs archived under `audit_evidence` for OT effectiveness reviews.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Lower `expected_fn_variety` in `nis2_ot_baseline.csv` for lab `cell_line=LINE-QA` so the live stats exceed 2× baseline; the search must return `anomaly=1` for that PLC/cell pair.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore baseline numbers or halt the injected traffic pattern and rerun over `-7d`; expect the QA line to disappear from the anomaly table when both ratios fall below thresholds.
